primary_areas.rationale: Ground entry node; contains conflicting count claims '(9 steps)' (line 9) and '(16 agents: 9 producer + 7 reviewer)' (line 10) that diverge from filesystem inventory (10 steps/ dirs, 16 agents).
Ground entry node; ls -1 enumerates 10 subdirectories including triage/, which AGENTS.md:9 'Step pool' phrasing excludes.

Ground entry node; ls -1 enumerates 16 agent files; matches AGENTS.md:10 total but AGENTS.md decomposition '9 producer + 7 reviewer' is internally inconsistent with the 16 listed (9 producer + 7 reviewer = 16 producer/reviewer pairs minus reflect/verify reviewers — needs interpretation).
Ground entry node; typecheck succeeded (exit 0), no test/lint scripts.

ripple.rationale: Ground neighbor; triage/ exists on filesystem but AGENTS.md:9 'Step pool' enumeration omits it (counts only 9 names) — discrepancy locus.
Ground neighbor; referenced in AGENTS.md:9 step pool.
Ground neighbor; referenced in AGENTS.md:9 step pool.
Ground neighbor; typechecks clean (Ground t6 exit 0).

source.command: count of dedup union(entry_nodes.path, neighbors.id) from Ground

constraints.statement: AGENTS.md is project canonical instruction file (per .blazewrit/CLAUDE.md 'See AGENTS.md'); count claims therein function as normative documentation.
constraints.evidence: Ground entry_nodes includes AGENTS.md as doc; CLAUDE.md content shown in system context.

constraints.statement: Filesystem inventory of steps/ (10 dirs) and .claude/agents/ (16 files) is ground truth via Ground tool_calls t3/t4/t2/t5 with identical run1/run2.
ED (emberdeck) snapshot unavailable; ED-degree-derived fields must be omitted, not substituted.

risk_surface.failure_mode: Documentation count drift: AGENTS.md:9 says '9 steps' but steps/ contains 10 entries including triage/.
risk_surface.evidence: Ground.conflicts.c1 raw_evidence (AGENTS.md:9 vs ls steps/ stdout).

risk_surface.failure_mode: Agent decomposition arithmetic inconsistency: AGENTS.md:10 claims '9 producer + 7 reviewer' totalling 16, but filesystem shows 16 files where producer count = 10 (decide, ground, implement, investigate, reflect, report, spec, test, verify — note no triage agent file) and reviewer count = 7 (no reflect-reviewer, no verify-reviewer, no triage-reviewer).
risk_surface.evidence: Ground.conflicts.c2 raw_evidence; counting agent files from t5 stdout.

risk_surface.failure_mode: Triage step's status ambiguous: directory exists (steps/triage/) but no triage agent file in .claude/agents/ — orchestration role of triage not surfaced in AGENTS.md step pool.
risk_surface.evidence: Ground t4 lists 'triage/'; Ground t5 lists no 'triage*.md' agent file; AGENTS.md:7 'None (free conversation) ↔ Triage → Flow[...]' positions Triage as a pre-flow stage.

risk_surface.failure_mode: Omission risk in count reporting: emit '9 steps' verbatim from AGENTS.md without filesystem reconciliation would propagate stale count downstream.

validity_check.ground_facts_summary: Ground provides: AGENTS.md:9 phrasing '(9 steps)'; AGENTS.md:10 phrasing '(16 agents: 9 producer + 7 reviewer — Verify/Reflect have no reviewer)'; ls steps/ enumerates 10 directories (decide ground implement investigate reflect report spec test triage verify); ls .claude/agents/ enumerates 16 files; typecheck exit 0; git HEAD stable at 4e74abd; working tree dirty on WORKFLOW_PLAN.md only.
validity_check.triage_intent_target: plan-standalone flow_type; interpret '9-step workflow architecture' referent against repo inventory.
validity_check.no_op_detection: Not a no-op: Ground.conflicts c1+c2 surface real divergence between documentation phrasing and filesystem inventory that requires interpretation.

issues.summary: Ambiguity: does '9-step workflow' refer to AGENTS.md:9 step pool (excludes triage) or filesystem inventory (10 dirs including triage)?
Agent decomposition (9 producer + 7 reviewer) vs filesystem listing requires explicit role mapping (triage has no agent file; reflect/verify have no reviewer).

ground_unknowns_addressed.rationale: Documentation/inventory flow; ED graph not required for interpretation of count claims.
